items.q: Which item is removed first from a stack?
items.why: Students confuse stacks (LIFO - like a stack of plates) with queues (FIFO - like a checkout line). The 'stack of plates' analogy makes LIFO concrete.

items.q: What is the difference between OR and XOR?
items.correct: OR is true when at least one input is true; XOR is true when exactly one input is true.
items.misconception: They are the same gate with different names.
items.random_wrong: OR is for numbers, XOR is for letters.
items.why: Students miss the critical case: when BOTH inputs are 1. OR gives 1, XOR gives 0. XOR is 'exclusive' - excludes the both-true case.

items.q: What is the time complexity of binary search?
items.misconception: O(n) - because it searches through items.
items.why: Students conflate 'fast' with O(1). Binary search halves the search space each step, giving logarithmic time - much better than linear, but not constant.

items.q: Which technique involves breaking a problem into smaller sub-problems?
items.why: Correct answer: Decomposition. “Abstraction” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the purpose of a trace table?
items.correct: To track variable values during execution
items.why: Correct answer: To track variable values during execution. “To compile code” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many distinct shapes are commonly used in basic flowcharts (start/end, process, decision, input/output)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 shapes. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
